_comment: Corpus manifest. Each module is benchmarked through every pipeline stage it supports; 'stages' (optional) limits which stages run. 'file' resolves to corpus/<file>; 'path' (optional, relative to bench/) references a wasm in place and is skipped when absent. 'init' (optional) names an export the host calls once after instantiate, before exec (wago has no start section, so AssemblyScript modules expose _initialize). Modules with 'exec' entries are also run end-to-end (args are i32). Three tiers: synthetic micro/scale modules (isolate one codegen aspect, regenerate .wat via corpus/build.sh), 'compute' kernels (real algorithms), and 'real'/'real-large' third-party programs (AssemblyScript libraries + the wasm3 interpreter; build via corpus/build-as.sh). The real programs need env.abort (a no-op host stub) and are committed so the suite needs no toolchain at run time.

modules.desc: singly-linked list build + dependent-load pointer chase
escape-time Mandelbrot over an n x n grid (f64 compute)
Sieve of Eratosthenes: memory + strided marking + branches
AssemblyScript blake-as: BLAKE3 hash (SWAR) of a 4 KiB buffer
AssemblyScript utf-as: UTF-8 -> UTF-16 transcode (SWAR) of a 4 KiB buffer
wasm3 interpreter (WASI); decode+validate only (host imports return values)
